section_title: INTRODUCTION TO OTE SERIES VOLUME 8: BRITISH POUND CABLE SETUP
content:
Welcome to the ICT Optimal Trade Entry Pattern Recognition Series volume number eight, featuring the
British Pound Cable.
The chart is set to the five-minute timeframe. For trading on Monday, disregard Sunday's trading and
consider Friday's higher and Friday's low, specifically within the New York session from 8:30 a.m. all the
way to 11 a.m. New York time.
This specific New York session area, which highlights specific setups for this model, is a wider window than
the typical ICT New York Kill Zone. It takes us up into a little bit towards the midpoint of London close and
the heart of New York open. This model was created to provide free education and avoid costly services.
ICT'

This video series is designed to share valuable trading concepts with the public, so you don't have to pay
for education, join chat rooms, or subscribe to signal services. The intention is to put those charging for
what is freely demonstrable out of business.
Addressing criticism regarding hindsight examples: Hindsight is how everyone starts. This series aims to
inspire and motivate individuals, as the industry can be daunting with much 'fluff' and 'frauds' on social
media, especially Instagram, who do not make money with trading. ICT aims to prove that these methods
are not only effective but also transferable.
This video series shows setups that work with very specific rule-based ideas around a specific time of day,
happening every single day, Monday through Friday.

section_title: UTILIZING THE FIBONACCI FOR OPTIMAL TRADE ENTRY (OTE)
content:
Looking at Friday's chart high, this line indicates that level. We are going to look at the optimal trade entry
here.
To identify the OTE:
- Take the Fibonacci tool and draw it from a key low to a swing high. This swing low is defined as a 'kilo'.
- Extend the fib out in time.
Observations on the Fibonacci projection:
- The price retracement down into the optimal trade entry is visible.
- The projection did not necessarily get to half of the standard deviation that makes up the fib range (the
dealing range), meaning it didn't reach 0.50. This is not a requirement.
- The fib is a ballpark figure; markets do not move on fibs. The Fibonacci just helps frame an idea that is
very visual to a new trader.
There are other market dynamics at play within this swing that do not require the Fibonacci. Most
mentorship students don't require the Fibonacci either. However, for a general audience or those new to
these concepts, the fib helps give a foundation to traders who can't readily see key price swings in a trend
or a fractal of price action.

section_title: LIVE TRADE EXECUTION AND TARGET IDENTIFICATION
content:
Price comes down and hits the optimal trade entry. The entry was made right on a specific candle.
- It's not imperative to get precisely inside that optimal trade entry; it's better if you can, but sometimes
circumstances (like talking to a son) might lead to a quick entry slightly outside the absolute perfect point.
- The idea behind the trade was that it was going to run this short-term high.
- Using the parameters for this YouTube model, the target was to reach an old high, specifically Monday's
high reaching to Friday's high. Price reaching up into it and the entire business is running above these
relative equal highs, indicating liquidity above that area.
This pattern and its appearance in hindsight, including the entered and exited candles, are displayed. A
recording of this trade is provided, showing the entry and the stop at 21.68, anticipating a sudden
expansion higher. This was shown using live data from TradingView, as MT4 has been abandoned due to
concerns about faked data and fraud.

Further commentary focuses on drawing attention to the relative equal highs. An order block could
potentially be tapped, but it's not strictly necessary; allowing for it to go that low would not change the
trade's validity. Price then runs up and expands above that high. This trade was also shared and looped on
Twitter.
ICT sometimes uses Twitter to draw attention to market movements that catch his eye, without giving
explicit entry, stop, or target details. If you're familiar with these concepts and a student of this video series,
you will understand the hints being provided.
ICT maintains a clear, one-sided trading approach: either right or wrong, promoting long-term consistency
through conviction.
